2026-07-06 14:07:35

Market - The market extended last week's constructive tone as investors continued to favor risk assets. Although day-to-day price action remained mixed, the broader trend stayed positive, allowing TurboRegime to remain comfortably in its newly established RiskOn regime. The strategy continues to participate in the market while maintaining a modest defensive allocation.

TReg - Three trading days into the current RiskOn regime, the trade has moved into positive territory with a gain of 0.37%. The maximum drawdown experienced since the transition has been limited to just -0.22%, reflecting a relatively smooth start to the new position. This early performance supports the model's decision to increase market exposure while avoiding the additional risk of leveraged positions.

Regime - TurboRegime remains in RiskOn, active since July 1. The current regime continues to perform as expected, with positive returns and minimal drawdown during its first three trading days. No portfolio changes are indicated at this time. The model will continue evaluating the strength of the underlying trend and market participation.  The strategy remains disciplined, making allocation changes only after confirmed end-of-day signals rather than reacting to intraday market noise.

Trades - TurboRegime has now been live since April 1, 2026, and has completed six live trades. During that time, the strategy has generated a live return of 32.03%, compared with 13.96% for a buy-and-hold investment in SPY over the same period, producing 18.07 percentage points of excess return.

The strategy has experienced a wide variety of market environments, including defensive RiskOff periods, traditional RiskOn allocations, and one leveraged RiskOn++ (Turbo) trade. The Turbo trade captured the strongest portion of the spring rally, returning 27.72% while SPY gained 9.34% during the same period.

Not every trade has been profitable, nor should investors expect every trade to outperform. Three of the six completed trades have produced positive returns, illustrating that TurboRegime is designed to manage an entire market cycle rather than optimize every individual trade. Recent examples include exiting a RiskOn position early to limit losses before moving defensive, followed by re-entering RiskOn as market conditions improved.

With only six completed live trades over 96 days, the strategy has already demonstrated one of its defining characteristics: remaining patient during uncertain conditions while becoming more aggressive only when the probability of sustained market strength increases. Every allocation change has been driven by objective end-of-day signals, avoiding emotional reactions to intraday volatility.
